summary: Social and charitable non-profit organization for English-speaking women in Madrid. Founded decades ago, active today with a clubhouse at Plaza de la República del Ecuador 6. Annual membership €88.
content: The American Women's Club of Madrid (AWC) is one of the longest-running English-speaking community organizations in Madrid. Open to all English-speaking women of any nationality (not just Americans), the club serves as a social hub, charitable organization, and support network for newly-arrived expats and long-term residents alike. Their clubhouse at Plaza de la República del Ecuador 6 hosts regular activities including book clubs, craft groups, walking tours, wine tastings, and conversation groups. The AWC is particularly well-known for its monthly secondhand sales, annual 5K walk for breast cancer awareness, and the holiday bazaar — all of which raise funds for local Madrid charities. For new arrivals in Madrid, joining the AWC is one of the fastest ways to build a social network of English-speaking women who understand the practical challenges of settling in Spain.

sections.heading: You do not need to be American
sections.body: Despite the name, AWC Madrid is open to all English-speaking women of any nationality. Current membership includes British, Irish, Canadian, Australian, South African, and Indian members, as well as Spanish women who speak English.

items.label: Clubhouse
items.value: Plaza de la República del Ecuador 6

items.title: Secondhand sales
items.body: Monthly charity sales at the clubhouse — books, clothing, household items. A staple of the Madrid expat calendar.

items.title: Annual 5K walk
items.body: Breast cancer awareness fundraiser held each autumn, open to non-members.

items.title: Holiday bazaar
items.body: Annual winter market with handmade crafts, baked goods, and expat-made products. Major fundraising event.

items.title: Book clubs & craft groups
items.body: Multiple recurring small-group activities, mostly in English.

items.title: Walking tours & day trips
items.body: Member-organized outings to Madrid neighborhoods, museums, and nearby towns.

sections.heading: Best for new arrivals
sections.body: If you have just moved to Madrid and are feeling isolated, the AWC's weekly coffee mornings and newcomer events are the single fastest way to meet other English-speaking women who have navigated the same challenges (NIE, healthcare, schools, finding an apartment). The club explicitly designs its calendar around welcoming new members.

sections.heading: Joining the AWC
do: Visit the clubhouse in person during a monthly secondhand sale to meet members before joining
Check awcmadrid.org/activities for the current month's calendar
Follow @awcmadrid on Instagram for last-minute event announcements
dont: Don't expect daily programming — most activities are weekly or monthly
Don't assume men can join — the AWC is women-only (see American Club of Madrid for mixed-gender alternative)

title: Devour Madrid Food Tours
summary: Award-winning English-language food tour company operating in Madrid since 2012. Co-founded by Lauren Aloise. Specializes in small-group tapas, market, and neighborhood food tours with English-speaking local guides.
content: Devour Madrid is the Madrid arm of Devour Tours, an English-language food tour company co-founded by Lauren Aloise in 2012. The company is one of the highest-rated tour operators in Madrid on TripAdvisor (5.0 stars from 8,000+ reviews) and has been featured in major English-language travel publications including Condé Nast Traveler, The Guardian, and BBC Travel. Devour differentiates itself from the free walking tour operators by being exclusively food-focused, capping groups small (usually 8-12 people), and using guides who are professional food writers or trained culinary historians rather than generalist guides. Their tours cover classic Madrid food neighborhoods (La Latina, Malasaña, Lavapiés) and themes (tapas, market tours, vermut culture, ham and wine). They also operate the popular 'Madrid Ultimate Spanish Cuisine Food Tour' sold via Viator. Devour Tours has since expanded to Barcelona, Seville, Lisbon, and Rome — but Madrid remains their flagship and founding city.

sections.heading: Why Devour stands out
sections.body: Devour Madrid is consistently the #1 rated food tour in Madrid on TripAdvisor for a reason: their guides are food writers and culinary historians, not generic tour guides. The tours are explicitly educational about Spanish food culture (what vermut is, why jamón ibérico costs what it costs, how tapas etiquette works) rather than just being a sequence of eating stops.

items.title: Tapas Crawl through La Latina
items.body: The classic — Sunday morning tapas tour through Madrid's most traditional tapas neighborhood.

items.title: Malasaña Food & History Tour
items.body: Combines Madrid's hipster neighborhood with stops at family-run shops and vermuterías.

items.title: Market Tour at Mercado de San Miguel
items.body: Guided visit to the iconic market with tastings at select stalls.

items.title: Ultimate Spanish Cuisine Tour
items.body: Half-day small-group tour combining multiple neighborhoods — sold via Viator.

items.title: Ham, Wine & Vermut Tasting
items.body: Specialty tasting tour focused on Spain's three iconic food/drink categories.

sections.heading: Booking tips
do: Book 1-2 weeks ahead in shoulder season (April–May, September–October)
Book 3-4 weeks ahead for peak season (June, July, September)
Tell your guide about food allergies at booking — they accommodate well but need notice
Tip 10-15% if you had a great time (Spanish tipping norm for excellent service)
dont: Don't expect a free tour — Devour is a premium operator, not a tips-based model
Don't book a morning tour on Sunday in La Latina without knowing it will be crowded with locals
Don't eat a big meal before the tour — most tours include 5-7 substantial tastings

title: Moving2Madrid — Relocation & Property Shopper
summary: Madrid-based relocation and property buyer's agent service specifically targeting international (non-Spanish) clients. Helps expats rent or buy property in Madrid with English-speaking support throughout the process.
content: Moving2Madrid is a boutique relocation and property shopper service explicitly built for international clients who do not yet speak Spanish and are not familiar with the Madrid real estate market. Unlike traditional Madrid inmobiliarias (real estate agencies), Moving2Madrid operates as a buyer's agent and tenant's agent — meaning they represent the client, not the landlord or seller. Their service spans the full relocation journey: neighborhood selection, property search, negotiation, contract review, NIE assistance, utility setup, school placement, and ongoing concierge support for the first months after move-in. They are particularly known for their content marketing (a deep blog with detailed English-language guides on Madrid neighborhoods, mortgage rules for non-residents, and the Spanish property buying process) which makes them a frequently-cited resource in expat Facebook groups. They are listed on Expatica Spain's directory and are recommended in r/GoingToSpain and multiple Madrid expat Facebook groups.

sections.heading: Why a buyer's agent matters in Madrid
sections.body: Spanish real estate agents legally represent the seller or landlord, not the buyer. Moving2Madrid fills the opposite role — they represent you. This matters most for international clients who don't speak Spanish fluently and don't know which neighborhoods fit their lifestyle and budget.

items.label: Pricing model
items.value: Flat fee or % of property price

items.title: Rental search
items.body: End-to-end rental assistance: neighborhood selection, viewings, negotiation, contract review, move-in support. Particularly useful for newcomers who cannot physically visit properties before arrival.

items.title: Buyer's agent
items.body: Property search, negotiation, legal coordination, mortgage broker introductions for non-resident buyers. Critical for non-Spanish buyers unfamiliar with the buying process.

items.title: NIE & residency support
items.body: Coordination of the NIE (foreigner ID number) process, which is required to sign any rental contract or property deed in Spain.

items.title: School placement
items.body: Advice on bilingual and international schools based on neighborhood selection. Often paired with family relocation packages.

items.title: Settling-in concierge
items.body: Utility setup, bank account opening, healthcare registration, mobile plan — the first-month admin avalanche.

sections.heading: When to use Moving2Madrid vs Idealista
do: Use Moving2Madrid if you are buying property and don't speak Spanish fluently
Use Moving2Madrid if you are relocating with a family and need end-to-end coordination
Use Idealista or Fotocasa if you already live in Madrid and just want to see listings
dont: Don't expect free services — they charge a flat fee or percentage, separate from any landlord commission
Don't confuse them with a listing platform — they don't publish a public property database

sections.heading: Alternative options
sections.body: Other English-speaking relocation services in Madrid include MADRIDEM (madridem.com), PropXper (propxper.com), and Help in Spain (spain-help.com). Compare quotes from 2-3 before committing — pricing models vary significantly.
